segments.integrated_caption: The video opens on a black screen, accompanied by gentle, melancholic acoustic guitar music. As the music continues, a male narrator with a deep, slightly accented voice introduces the segment, stating, "With a huge talent and big ambitions, that's how Cristiano Ronaldo started his career." This narration accompanies a stylized montage of Cristiano's early life, beginning with a black-and-white photo of a young Cristiano in an 'AQUIMAR' jersey, set against a family portrait and a scenic church on a rocky hill. The visual transitions to a sepia-toned image of a teenage Cristiano in a green-and-white striped jersey, with a younger version and an action shot beside him, all against a grand architectural background. The narrator continues, emphasizing that "talent alone is never enough," and lists "hard work, strong will, self-confidence, and a strong personality" as key factors. During this, a red-tinted image of Cristiano in a Manchester United jersey celebrating appears, followed by a split-screen showing a young Cristiano with a whistle and a mature Cristiano holding a large silver trophy. The narrator concludes, "This is the story of Cristiano's beginnings and how he got to the pinnacle of football." The montage then shifts to vibrant real-action footage, showing a close-up of a young Cristiano in a blue training environment, followed by dynamic clips of him playing in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, and scoring a goal. As he scores and celebrates with teammates, an excited commentator exclaims, "Oh my goodness, what a goal! And he scores the goal!" followed by another commentator shouting, "Ronaldo!" The video culminates with Cristiano in a suit holding the golden Ballon d'Or trophy, a title card displaying 'balón' in red, and finally, the bold white text 'CRISTIANO ASCENDING TO GREATNESS' on a black background, as the acoustic guitar music fades out.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a black screen, then transitions into a stylized montage of Cristiano Ronaldo’s life and career. It begins with a young Cristiano in a black-and-white photo, wearing a white and black sports jersey with 'AQUIMAR' printed on it, standing in front of a family portrait and a scenic backdrop of a church on a rocky hill. The scene shifts to a sepia-toned image of a teenage Cristiano in a green-and-white striped jersey, with a younger version of himself and a playing action shot beside him, set against a grand architectural background. The next frame shows a red-tinted image of Cristiano in a Manchester United jersey, celebrating with an open mouth, flanked by two other versions of himself in similar poses. This is followed by a split-screen image: on the left, a young Cristiano in a black-and-white jersey with a whistle in his mouth; on the right, a mature Cristiano in a red jersey holding a large silver trophy. The montage then cuts to real-action footage: a close-up of a young Cristiano in a blue training environment, followed by a dynamic sequence of him playing in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, scoring a goal, and celebrating with teammates. The final frames show him in a suit holding the Ballon d'Or trophy, then a title card with the word 'balón' in red, and finally, the text 'CRISTIANO ASCENDING TO GREATNESS' in bold white letters on a black background. The visual style evolves from nostalgic, textured stills to vibrant, high-energy live-action clips, culminating in a dramatic title reveal.

audio_updated_entities.Cristiano Ronaldo: Appears in multiple stages of life, from a young boy in an 'AQUIMAR' jersey to a mature man in a suit holding the Ballon d'Or trophy. segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a black screen displaying the glowing, pixelated text 'MADEIRA 1985 - 1997', setting a nostalgic tone, accompanied by gentle, melancholic acoustic guitar music. A male narrator begins, stating that on the islands of Madeira, particularly in Funchal, Cristiano took his first steps towards a footballing career. Vintage photographs of Cristiano Ronaldo’s early life appear, showing him as a baby held by his parents, then as a young boy in a white suit in a family portrait with his mother in a red-and-white striped dress and father in a patterned shirt, with a white church on a rocky hill in the background. The narrator explains that Cristiano focused very early on football instead of studying, and due to his family's relatively poor background, his mother supported his dreams. The visual narrative then shifts to his early football journey, showing a close-up of his regional football license from 'Clube Futebol Andorinha' with his name and the years '92-95' highlighted. A team photo follows, showing a young Cristiano in a blue jersey seated in the front row with his youth team. The narrator confirms Cristiano played for Andorinha from 1992 to 1995, where his father worked as a kitman. An interview with Fernao de Sousa, identified as Cristiano’s godfather, appears in a sunlit outdoor setting. The narrator reveals that news of young Cristiano reached Fernando Soze, the youth coach of Nacional and Ronaldo's godfather, who played a significant role in his early career decisions, especially given the absence of his father Denis, who 'drank away his sorrows'. A photo of a teenage Cristiano with his father is shown, followed by the dark, stylized logo of Marítimo Madeira, and then a monochrome shield logo, likely representing Nacional. The narrator concludes by mentioning that despite attempts by Marítimo, the biggest club in Madeira, to sign Ronaldo, Nacional...

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a vintage-style still image of a young boy in a black-and-white 'AQUIMADEIRA' football jersey, standing beside a monochrome Club Logo (Shield with Stripes). An animated graphic appears, illustrating that "two sets of kits and 20 footballs was the sealer to get Cristiano, who was only 10 years old," as the narrator explains his early value. This transitions to grainy, archival footage of youth football matches, showing the boy actively running and playing on a dirt pitch in the same black-and-white kit. The narrator details how "at Nacional, Cristiano had his first taste of a real football academy," where coaches were "worried because of Cristiano's undernourishment" and asked his mother to provide a diet of "plenty of fish and meat," though "the talent was there." The boy is then seen in a Youth Football Team Photo, crouching in the front row with teammates in 'EUROPA' jerseys. The footage continues with dynamic match highlights, showing a player scoring and celebrating, followed by the boy running in a white t-shirt and blue shorts. The narrator emphasizes Cristiano's dedication, stating that "whereas a typical kid at his age would train for two hours a day, Cristiano used to train with the ball between 10 to 12 hours daily" due to having "no school, homework, and no house chores to worry about." This intense training meant that "within a couple of years, Cristiano became too big for the island," and his "next step was a matter of time." The scene shifts to a present-day setting, showing João de Freitas, a man in a suit, pointing at a Poster with a golden Ballon d'Or Trophy. The narrator reveals that "Cristiano's godfather played a big role again as he mentioned Ronaldo's name to João de Freitas, who was the president of the Sporting Fan Club in Madeira." João de Freitas is then seen walking along a Coastal Waterfront Setting with a scenic view of a coastal town and the Church on Rocky Hill, reinforcing the Madeira setting, before the segment ends with a wide shot of the cityscape. Throughout, a consistent, slightly dramatic background music plays.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a title card introducing Aurelio Pereira, Chief Scout of Sporting CP, set against a dark background with the club’s green crest, as a dramatic instrumental track begins. A narrator immediately identifies Aurelio Pereira as the lead scout for Sporting Lisbon, responsible for bringing in talents like Futre, Figo, Quaresma, Nani, and Moutinho, whose iconic images are displayed in a dynamic collage. The scene transitions to an outdoor setting where Aurelio Pereira, an older man in a green sweater, walks past a large white lion statue, symbolizing Sporting’s emblem. The narrator explains that the scouting process was simple and based on intuition, leading Pereira to give Cristiano a chance. A split-screen graphic then appears, showing the logos of Nacional (a dark blue shield with vertical stripes) and Sporting’s green crest, with a prominent '€25000' transfer fee. Two young boys, one in a white-and-black striped jersey (representing Cristiano from Nacional) and another in a green-and-white striped jersey (representing a Sporting youth player), are shown beneath the fee. The narrator clarifies that Nacional owed Sporting Lisbon €25,000 from an old transfer and offered Cristiano to cover the debt, despite the high price for a 12-year-old academy player. The video cuts to Aurelio Pereira walking across a sunlit football pitch, followed by a close-up of his feet stepping on the white line, as the narrator describes Cristiano flying out of Madeira alone to Lisbon. A panoramic cityscape with a river and historic buildings is overlaid with a faded image of a youth football match, where a young player in a green-and-white striped jersey dribbles past an opponent in a red-and-white striped jersey, with 'VULCANO' visible on a background banner. The narrator concludes by stating that what caught Aurelio and the coaches' eyes was Cristiano's personality, not just his talent, as Aurelio Pereira is shown sitting in a dugout, looking directly at the camera with a serious expression.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Aurelio Pereira, an older man with a mustache and glasses, seated in a modern dugout, as a narrator explains that Aurelio agreed to Cristiano's permanent stay at Sporting's Academy in the summer of '97. A dramatic instrumental score begins as the scene transitions to a split-screen, showing Pereira on the left and a young boy in a white-and-black striped jersey, likely young Cristiano, eating a snack on the right. A glowing title card 'SPORTING 1997 - 2003' appears, followed by aerial and ground-level shots of Lisbon, including a prominent statue and fountain. The narrator details Cristiano's 'huge difficulties' at Sporting's Academy, highlighting the different accent in the capital compared to Madeira, his placement in an older age group, and living far from his family. Visuals of a young Cristiano Ronaldo stretching on a grass field, standing seriously behind a goal net, and training with teammates in green kits accompany this narrative. The narrator continues, explaining that despite the ease of returning to Madeira, Cristiano 'stayed in the capital Lisbon to achieve his dreams,' as archival footage shows him sweeping a street with a broom. The segment concludes with a group photo of a youth football team, featuring a focused and determined young Cristiano Ronaldo in the back row, while the narrator describes his 'huge focus in training and his competitive spirit,' alongside 'many issues off the field,' particularly with academic studies and a discovered 'racing heart' condition.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a black-and-white group photo of a youth football team in green-and-white striped jerseys, featuring a focused Young Cristiano Ronaldo in the back row, marking his early integration into the Sporting CP youth academy. A calm male narrator explains how Cristiano developed from an individual to a team player, leading to his first professional contract. The scene transitions to archival footage of young boys in green Reebok hoodies walking, followed by a close-up of a young Ronaldo in a green training kit, showcasing his early dedication. A split-screen then shows two images of young Ronaldo in Sporting CP jerseys, kneeling with a trophy and holding a stone football trophy, emphasizing his early achievements. Text overlays confirm 'APRIL 2001' as the date of his first professional contract, introduced alongside the Sporting CP crest. The narrator identifies José Veiga as the 'most famous Portuguese agent' at the time, detailing the '4-YEAR DEAL' for '€2,000 PER MONTH', and revealing a hefty €20 million release clause. The video then transitions to a close-up of Ronaldo’s face with '2001-2002' overlaid, as the narrator describes how Cristiano, at 16, became financially capable of moving his mother to Lisbon and got closer to first-team football. Scenes follow of him walking with a man near a stadium and training in a blue Reebok jersey. The segment concludes with a formal portrait of László Bölöni, labeled 'SPORTING MANAGER', with the narrator identifying him as the 'Romanian manager' who called Ronaldo up to train with the first team. Throughout, a steady, narrative background music plays.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a formal portrait of László Bölöni, identified as 'SPORTING MANAGER', standing beside a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a Reebok tracksuit, set against a dramatic, slightly melancholic orchestral score. A handwritten 'Report: Cristiano Ronaldo 2001-2002' then appears on lined paper, as a narrator details its critical findings: "weak aerial ability, uses weak technique, defensively weak, physically weak, doesn't have tactical awareness as an individual nor as a team player, very selfish, and lacks composure and focus." Archival footage shows Ronaldo training with teammates in Reebok gear, jogging and running drills on a grassy field under overcast skies, including a close-up of Bölöni giving instructions to Ronaldo during a rainy session. The narrator explains that "after months and months of training, Cristiano managed to settle in with the first team in the training sessions," but Bölöni "still thought it was not the right time for him to play in official matches," as Ronaldo was "still showing off some tricks that had no place in actual matches, let alone in training sessions," and Bölöni "even threatened not to call up Ronaldo if he didn't stop." The music shifts to a more triumphant tone as the scene transitions to a celebratory moment with Ronaldo and teammates on a 'LISBOA VISION' bus, surrounded by cheering fans waving green-and-white flags, indicating a victory parade. The narrator confirms, "Sporting successfully won the league, the cup, and the Super Cup that season," with Bölöni integrating youngsters like Quaresma and Viana, but "Ronaldo was still waiting for his chance." Key figures Nuno Gomes and Rui Costa are introduced in a split-screen with the Sporting CP crest, followed by a locker room scene where Ronaldo is physically confronted by a teammate, suggesting internal conflict. The segment concludes with a dramatic introduction of Jorge Mendes, shown in a dark suit and sunglasses with his name displayed prominently against a green grid background, as the narrator states, "As the season had closed its curtains, a Portuguese agent called Jorge Mendes..."

The timeline expands to '2002-2003', and the narrator explains that Sporting's main striker, Mario Jardel, seen in a Sporting CP jersey, intended to leave. RONALDO' and the number 28, as the narrator reflects on his promising start, acknowledging that while he didn't start consistently, it was normal for a 17-year-old and enough to attract attention.

A male narrator's voice begins, explaining how Ronaldo attracted the attention of several European clubs and how his agent, Jorge Mendes, warned Sporting that Ronaldo would leave if not a starter. The scene transitions to a stylized graphic sequence featuring a smiling man in a suit, identified as the narrator, with logos of major European clubs—Juventus, Inter, Real Madrid, Liverpool, Arsenal, Barcelona, and Valencia—appearing behind him. Text overlays quote Ronaldo, stating he's 'very pleased to hear many important clubs are interested in me' and that his 'dream is to play in Spain or England,' a sentiment directly echoed by the narrator who quotes the 'Portuguese teenager' expressing his pleasure and dreams. The focus then shifts to Paco Roig, a candidate for Valencia presidency, shown in a suit with the Valencia CF crest, alongside a displayed €9.00M transfer fee, as the narrator details Roig's agreement with Mendes to sign both Cristiano and Quaresma for 9 million euros if he won the presidency. Next, Gérard Houllier, labeled as Liverpool Manager, appears with the Liverpool FC crest and a €8.00M fee, with Ronaldo in a red Portugal jersey, as the narrator confirms Liverpool's interest and their 8 million euro offer. The visuals maintain a dark, textured background with grid patterns, emphasizing the high-stakes transfer market, while the narrator concludes by quoting Ronaldo's reaction to Liverpool's offer, calling them 'one of the best clubs in Europe.'

The final frames focus on Barcelona's interest, showing a man in a suit with glasses pointing against a purple background with the FC Barcelona crest, while the narrator explains Barcelona was interested in Jorge Mendes's clients, including Quaresma, Tiago, Deco, and Ronaldo, but ultimately 'pushed to sign Quaresma and offered a preferential contract,' as framed photos of these players, with a focus on Quaresma and Cristiano, are sequentially displayed.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a split-screen graphic featuring framed photos of young Cristiano Ronaldo and Quaresma, set against an FC Barcelona crest, while a dramatic, investigative background music plays. A narrator begins by stating that despite advanced talks for Cristiano, it 'amounted to nothing.' The scene transitions to a dark red backdrop with the Arsenal crest, where Jorge Mendes, a man in a dark suit, appears smiling, accompanied by an overlay quote attributed to him: 'THERE WAS A TIME WHEN I REALLY THOUGHT HE WAS GOING TO ARSENAL.' The narrator confirms Mendes's statement, highlighting Arsenal as the club with the most advanced talks. The timeline shifts to 'JAN 2003,' showing Ronaldo’s photo alongside his mother’s photo and the Arsenal crest, with Arsène Wenger’s photo then added, as the narrator details Cristiano and his mother flying to London to visit Arsenal's training facility and meeting Wenger and Thierry Henry. A full-screen image of Ronaldo in a red Portugal jersey appears next to the book cover 'Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography' by Guillem Balagué, with a quote: 'CRISTIANO FELL IN LOVE WITH THE CLUB.' Balagué, a man with a beard, is then shown smiling next to another quote from him: 'THE PHILOSOPHY AND THE CARE WITH WHICH YOUNG PLAYERS ARE TREATED,' with the narrator attributing these insights to Ronaldo's biography. A diagram then connects Wenger, David Dein, and João de Freitas to the Arsenal crest, followed by a red Arsenal jersey displaying 'RONALDO 9' between Wenger and Ronaldo’s photo, as the narrator reveals Arsenal had agreed on all terms with Sporting and Wenger even kept the number 9 shirt for Cristiano. The scene shifts to a green background with the Sporting CP crest, showing Dein and Freitas in framed photos with a jagged white line symbolizing a broken connection, as the narrator explains that Arsenal's vice president David Dein went to Lisbon to seal the deal but offered too low a price for Sporting. The segment concludes with aerial footage of London, focusing on the Emirates Stadium and surrounding urban landscape, while the narrator mentions Arsenal's financial restrictions due to the construction of the new Emirates Stadium.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with an aerial view of Emirates Stadium, followed by a bold title card, 'THE UNITED STORY SUMMER 2003', in glowing red text on a black background. A subtle, dramatic orchestral score plays as a male narrator begins, discussing an English club's successful negotiations. The scene transitions to archival football footage of a young Cristiano Ronaldo, wearing a green-and-white striped Sporting CP jersey with 'PT' sponsorship, competing against a Manchester United player in a red 'vodafone' jersey. A referee's whistle sound briefly cuts through the music. The narrator explains that while many believe Ronaldo's transfer was due to his performance against the 'Red Devils' in a friendly in August 2003, there was significant prior planning from United. A red-toned graphic displaying '2002-2003' appears as the narrator details how, in that season, Sir Alex Ferguson's assistant, the Portuguese Carlos Queiroz, received reports on Cristiano and knew the club needed to act quickly. Carlos Queiroz is then shown in a red training top, identified as 'ASSISTANT MANAGER - UNITED', and his calm, slightly accented voice is heard stating, "I didn't take on any responsibilities in terms of recruiting young players, but I informed Alex about Cristiano." The video then juxtaposes Queiroz with Sir Alex Ferguson, highlighting their relationship. A flashback shows Queiroz in a green jacket with the Sporting CP crest and the years '94-96', followed by him on the bench writing notes, as the narrator explains Queiroz was Sporting Lisbon's manager during that period and used his connections to arrange a special agreement for exchanging methodologies and ideas. The segment concludes with a split-screen graphic showing the Manchester United and Sporting CP crests with bidirectional arrows, symbolizing this transfer relationship.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a split-screen graphic displaying the Manchester United and Sporting CP crests with bidirectional arrows, set against a backdrop of tense, dramatic instrumental music, as a narrator explains how certain deals would become much easier. The scene transitions to Jim Ryan, Reserve Team Coach at United, standing beside Sir Alex Ferguson, who gestures thoughtfully. Ryan, in a clear, slightly older male voice, is heard stating, "I've seen a player... I think he's a winger, but he's playing as a central striker with the youth team," followed by "I wouldn't wait long to declare our interest. There will be other clubs after him," a quote also displayed as text overlay. The narration continues, detailing how Ferguson managed to send Ryan to Sporting Lisbon's facilities and later suggested signing Cristiano Ronaldo to Sporting's management, with a condition of a loan-back, a common request from other competing clubs like Juventus, Real Madrid, Liverpool, Arsenal, Barcelona, and Valencia, whose crests appear in a montage. Ferguson is then seen on a phone call, with a photo of young Cristiano Ronaldo beside him, an arrow pointing to the Sporting CP crest, signifying United's pursuit. The narrator explains that a special agreement between Sporting Lisbon and Manchester United led to booking a friendly match to celebrate the opening of Sporting's new stadium, Estádio José Alvalade, a 3D model of which is shown with the date August 6, 2003. The video cuts to a real-life moment of Ferguson and Ronaldo walking near a bridge, then back to a graphic of the two clubs facing off at the stadium. The final frames show a split-screen of Ferguson and Jorge Mendes, identified by the narrator as the man in sunglasses, on August 5, 2003, underscoring the critical timing of their meeting a day before the friendly, as United's interest in Cristiano intensified.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dramatic, stylized graphic featuring Sir Alex Ferguson's face in a white-bordered frame against a textured red background with a grid overlay, setting a serious, strategic tone. A narrator's voice details Manchester United's past transfer misses, including Beckham, Veron, and Ronaldinho, before stating, 'So now let's see what's on the table.' The scene evolves into a split-screen format, with the left side displaying the Sporting Portugal crest and a green background, while the right side remains red. Framed portraits appear on the left: first, Jorge Mendes in a dark suit, followed by José Veiga in a light shirt. On the right, Ferguson’s portrait reappears, now under the Manchester United crest, followed by a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a green-and-white Sporting CP jersey. A third figure, a Man in Sunglasses (Sporting CP Representative) in a dark suit holding a phone, appears on the far right, symbolizing the negotiation side. The narrator explains that 'Sporting knew that Ronaldo and Mendes were not interested in renewing their deal,' despite new manager Fernando Santos pledging to play Cristiano as a starter. The graphic transitions to real-world footage: Ferguson smiling on a sunny training ground, then being interviewed by reporters on the pitch, wearing a grey Manchester United training shirt with 'vodafone' sponsorship. The narrator adds that 'Ferguson and United wanted to inject some new blood into the team, and Cristiano filled that bill.' The narration continues, stating that 'Mendes knew that sealing this deal would catapult him to the forefront of all football deals,' and that 'the deal made sense for each side,' with only 'the figure' missing, as Ferguson 'knew that United can outbid any other club.' The scene cuts to grainy archival footage of Ronaldo in action—celebrating a goal in a black-and-white Sporting CP kit, then playing in a green-and-white striped jersey, dribbling past opponents and scoring, with the ball hitting the net, and visible sponsor banners like 'Coca-Cola' and 'Varilux' in the background. Over this, the narrator reveals that 'Cristiano knew that the deal was agreed before the booked friendly,' and he 'entered the match to show Manchester United what they were getting.' Live commentators then enthusiastically describe Ronaldo's play, praising a 'lovely little trick that he has' and noting how he 'swings one,' with an opponent 'couldn't get near him,' all underscored by energetic instrumental music.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with archival football match footage showing a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a beige and black Sporting CP jersey (number 28) skillfully dribbling past opponents in red jerseys on a green pitch, as a commentator praises his 'unbelievably quick feet' and declares he's 'gonna be a superstar.' The scene transitions to a close-up of Ronaldo in the same jersey, followed by a quote from Sir Alex Ferguson overlaid on a red grid background: 'THE BIGGEST SURGE OF EXCITEMENT AND ANTICIPATION I EXPERIENCED IN FOOTBALL MANAGEMENT,' which a narrator confirms was Ferguson's reaction to seeing Ronaldo play live. The next frame shows Ferguson in a suit, seated on the bench with a focused expression, flanked by players. This is followed by a quote from Carlos Freitas: 'THEIR PRIORITIES CHANGED AND THEY SAID 'WE'LL PAY MORE, BUT HE'S COMING STRAIGHT AWAY',' displayed over a dark green grid background, as the narrator identifies Freitas as the director of football who confirmed United's decision to pay more for an immediate transfer. A graphic then appears with Ronaldo in a Portugal jersey (number 7) and a transfer fee of '£12.24M' alongside a timeline from 03/04 to 07/08, with a salary of '€24,000' shown below, which the narrator elaborates was a final price of 12.2 million pounds for a five-year contract, with Ronaldo's yearly salary increasing to 2 million euros. Another Ferguson quote follows: 'NEXT YEAR YOU'LL PLAY IN HALF THE GAMES,' as he is shown smiling while on a phone call, with the narrator confirming this was Ferguson's promise regarding Ronaldo's playing time. The final frames show Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey (number 7) being interviewed, with a microphone held close to him, and then walking away from the camera, his back to the viewer, as he moves through a stadium with red seats, symbolizing his arrival at the club after initially asking for number 28 but being given number 7.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo’s back, wearing a red Manchester United jersey with 'RONALDO' and the number 7, as he walks away from the camera. A narrator speaks about the "honor of wearing the number 7 shirt at United," a "legendary number." This transitions into a stylized graphic with the number 7, overlaid with black-and-white portraits of past United legends—Coppell, Best, Robson, and Becks—as the narrator explicitly names "George Best, Steve Coppell, Brian Robson, Eric Cantona, and David Beckham" as those who wore it, emphasizing the jersey's legacy. The scene shifts to a smiling Ronaldo, arm-in-arm with a teammate in a red training kit, set against the backdrop of a stadium with red seats, as the narrator discusses "a huge transformation in Cristiano's life." A black screen follows, then a glowing title card reads 'UNITED 2003 -', while the background music becomes more rhythmic and driving. The narrator explains that "United were four-time league champions" but "Ferguson thought that the team needed a bit of youthful energy." This leads into a celebratory moment where fans wave a 'CHAMPIONS' banner, followed by Sir Alex Ferguson lifting the Premier League trophy amidst confetti, and Ronaldo in a red jersey celebrating with teammates. A formal photo shows Ronaldo, Ferguson, and a teammate posing together in front of a Manchester United backdrop. The narrator details that "four days after Cristiano was introduced to the media, he was given his debut" against Bolton, with "excitement in the stands" palpable, accompanied by a crowd cheering sound. The video then cuts to Ronaldo in a red jersey walking on the pitch, followed by a close-up of his face as he runs, as the narrator states "Cristiano managed to win a penalty for the team and was voted the player of the match." The final frames show Ronaldo in a Portugal national team jersey (number 11) during a match, with a focused expression under stadium lights, as the narrator concludes that "Luis Scolari gave Cristiano his national team debut with Portugal as well."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a black-and-white shot of a young Cristiano Ronaldo, number 7, alongside another player in Manchester United training gear, as a male narrator with a serious tone describes Cristiano's promising start but notes his teammates were not convinced of his playstyle, leading to him receiving tough, physical treatment and reacting negatively by diving and flopping. The visuals transition to a focused close-up of Ronaldo in a white training top, then dynamic match footage of him in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, shooting, and being tackled, illustrating the narrator's point that he earned a reputation for wanting to show off and needing to drastically change his playstyle. The scene shifts to a stylized, grainy retro-style screen displaying match highlights from his first season with United, which the narrator introduces as "bad and good examples," all set against a backdrop of dramatic instrumental music. We see a 'WOLVES - UNITED, JAN 17, 2004' title card followed by Ronaldo scoring a powerful goal, which the narrator critiques as a "speculative shot from distance" while United were losing. Another clip shows Ronaldo being tackled, described as demonstrating his "great pace but weak physical strength" as a defender pushes him out. The narrative then highlights positive examples, with a 'UNITED - FULHAM, OCT 25, 2003' title card preceding Ronaldo scoring a free-kick goal, praised as "a great free kick with a different technique than the one he uses today." Further clips include 'UNITED - CHELSEA, SEP 21, 2003', where Ronaldo displays "great bit of skill to escape Chelsea's press," and 'UNITED - ARSENAL, SEP 21, 2003', showing him dribbling past a defender, which the narrator begins to describe as a "great bit of" skill before the audio cuts off.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with archival match footage from September 21, 2003, showing a young Cristiano Ronaldo in his red Manchester United number 7 jersey skillfully dribbling past an Arsenal defender, though the narrator notes his "skill to escape Arsenal's pressure" was "followed by a poor pass forward" amidst the sounds of a football match. This visual narrative of mentorship is underscored by a split-screen of Roy Keane and Sir Alex Ferguson embracing Ronaldo, with the narrator explaining that "United had a completely different philosophy, but Ferguson treated the teenager just like he treated Cantona in the past, different from the other players." A close-up of Ferguson embracing Ronaldo in a white training top further emphasizes this connection. A quote from Roy Keane, displayed over a red grid background with his image, states: 'He was immediately one of the hardest working players at United,' a sentiment echoed by the narrator who adds that "Fergie knew that despite his lack of tactical awareness and teamwork, Cristiano had the right mindset for any professional." The narrative shifts to Ronaldo scoring his first goal against Portsmouth, with on-screen text 'UNITED 3-0 P'MOUTH, RONALDO - 80’', as a commentator excitedly announces, "Cristiano Ronaldo's first goal for United!" and the narrator confirms it came "through a free kick against Portsmouth." A graphic then highlights Ronaldo’s early stats: 'UNTIL JANUARY, 19 CAPS, 11 STARTS', with the narrator confirming he "managed to participate in 19 matches, starting in 11," followed by a shot of Sir Alex Ferguson clapping. The segment concludes with the narrator explaining that "United were in a transitional period that season," having "crashed out of the round of 16 of the Champions League against Porto and Mourinho," with a commentator's excited shout of "It's been turned in by Costinha!" likely referencing that Champions League exit. Despite this, a graphic shows the EPL 03/04 standings with United in third place, and Ronaldo's growing impact is reinforced by a quick shot of him scoring a goal against Chelsea, as the narrator states "In the league, United ended the season in third position."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an EPL 03/04 standings graphic, highlighting Manchester United in third place with 75 points, behind Arsenal and Chelsea, as a male narrator explains United finished behind "an improved Chelsea side and Arsenal, who remained unbeaten throughout the season." The scene transitions to a warm embrace between Sir Alex Ferguson and a young Cristiano Ronaldo in his Manchester United #7 jersey, symbolizing their mentor-mentee relationship. A tactical formation graphic then shows United's 4-4-2 setup with Ronaldo on the right wing, alongside players like Van Nistelrooy, Scholes, and Keane, as the narrator explains "United got all the way to the FA Cup final as they faced Millwall from the Championship. Ferguson decided to use Cristiano from the start to give him a taste of competing for a trophy." The narrative shifts to live match footage, showing Ronaldo in his red United jersey, with the narrator remarking on his improved performance: "The struggles of the season were behind him, as he looked much better. It could be one of his most important matches in his career, as he was just outstanding." A male commentator then exclaims, "Oh, a wonderful start from the Portuguese young international!" amidst crowd noise, before the narrator states Ronaldo "scored the goal" as live match footage shows him scoring against Millwall in the 44th minute, with the commentator shouting "Ronaldo!" and the crowd roaring in excitement. A title card confirms '2004 FA CUP FINAL UNITED 1-0 MILLWALL RONALDO - 44'. This is followed by a celebration with Ferguson and the team lifting the FA Cup trophy, with the narrator confirming, "As United won the match handsomely by three goals to nil." The commentator adds, "It's United's year for the FA Cup again," as a title card displays 'UNITED 3-0 MILLWALL 16 FULL TIME'. The segment then transitions to a black screen with glowing text 'EURO SUMMER 2004', accompanied by ambient, slightly melancholic music. A portrait of Luiz Scolari appears as the narrator explains, "Luis Scolari gave Cristiano some national team caps before the Euros, which were held in Portugal that summer." The Portugal squad list is shown, with Ronaldo highlighted as number 17, followed by footage of him training with the national team, laughing and interacting with teammates. The narrator continues, "And Cristiano successfully joined Scolari's final squad for the summer tournament and became the sixth youngest player in the competition's history. He arrived at the training camp full of confidence." The video concludes with a quote from Scolari: 'He’s a good kid, but someone must’ve told him he’s the best in the world,' underscoring the early perception of Ronaldo’s confidence and talent.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dramatic, slightly accented male narrator quoting Luiz Scolari, stating, 'As Scolari said, that he's a good kid, but someone must have told him he's the best in the world. And if he thinks he is, it will be very hard to work with him.' This quote is displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid background, accompanied by Scolari’s portrait. Epic, dramatic background music begins as the scene transitions to a montage of Cristiano Ronaldo in his Portugal national team jersey (number 17, red with green trim, Nike logo, and a yellow armband). The narrator explains that 'Cristiano was gaining more minutes as the tournament progressed.' Flags of Greece, Russia, and Spain appear sequentially, with the narrator detailing match results: Portugal 1-2 Greece, where Ronaldo entered in the second half, gave away a penalty, and scored, but Portugal lost; Russia 0-2 Portugal, where Ronaldo assisted; and Spain 0-1 Portugal, where Ronaldo started and was named man of the match. Brief clips of match action show Ronaldo celebrating, frustrated, or intense. The sequence continues with Portugal 2-1 England (penalty shootout 6-5), where Ronaldo started the quarter-final, and visually, Portugal 2-1 Ukraine, though the narrator mentions a win against the Netherlands in the semi-finals where Ronaldo assisted and scored, with Ronaldo shown shirtless in celebration. A black-and-white team photo of the Portugal squad is shown, with Ronaldo in the front row, as the narrator states he became the youngest player ever to start a final. The segment ends with a clip of the 0-1 loss to Greece, where Ronaldo is seen dejected and screaming in frustration, as the narrator concludes that the final ended like the opener, with Greece winning and Ronaldo missing a huge chance and being very upset. The dramatic music swells throughout.

The sequence continues with Portugal 2-1 England (penalty shootout 6-5) and Portugal 2-1 Ukraine, with Ronaldo shown shirtless in celebration. A black-and-white team photo of the Portugal squad is shown, with Ronaldo in the front row, followed by a clip of a 0-1 loss to Greece, where Ronaldo is seen dejected. The segment ends with Ronaldo screaming in frustration, emphasizing the emotional highs and lows of his Euro 2004 campaign.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dramatic black-and-white image of Cristiano Ronaldo screaming in frustration during a Euro 2004 match, a '0-1' score and Greek flag hinting at a loss, set against a somber, dramatic background music. A clear, authoritative male narrator begins, explaining that "Cristiano came back to United a different person." The scene transitions to a close-up of a younger, confident Ronaldo in a Manchester United training top (number 10). The narrator then quotes Mike Phelan, whose words are simultaneously displayed as white text on a dark red background: "His body shape was different, his facial features were different... That’s an athletic physique for the British game." Footage of Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship, running during training, showcases his athleticism. The narrator continues, noting Ronaldo's performances remained inconsistent, which weighed on Gary Neville, whose quote is then displayed and spoken by the narrator: "Do you live with him for another 12 months? Where he’s doing OK but not scoring 25 goals? Where he can turn off and not defend?" This is intercut with grainy archival match footage from 2004-2005 against Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton, where Ronaldo is seen dribbling, shooting, and scoring, with on-screen graphics indicating match details. The narrator describes Ronaldo's continued focus on "new skills" despite "wrong decisions" but also his "slight improvements" in passing. The segment concludes with the narrator stating Ronaldo "now has a rocket of a shot," immediately followed by an excited male commentator exclaiming "Now he's got!" and a crowd cheer, reinforcing the dynamic play seen in the match highlights.

This is followed by footage of Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship, running during training, showcasing his athleticism. Where he can turn off and not defend?' This is intercut with archival match footage from 2004-2005, including games against Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton, where Ronaldo is seen dribbling, shooting, and scoring, with on-screen graphics indicating the match dates and scores. The footage highlights his dynamic play and goal-scoring ability, reinforcing the narrative of his rising talent and the growing scrutiny around his defensive responsibilities.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a montage of archival football match highlights from the 2004-2005 season, presented on a retro-style screen with a red border, showcasing Cristiano Ronaldo in action for Manchester United against Bolton. As Ronaldo scores, a male narrator exclaims, "the goal! And what a goal!" amidst crowd cheering. More highlights show Ronaldo's dynamic play against Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton, with on-screen graphics detailing match dates and scores. The narrator comments on Ronaldo's skill, noting, "And on his weak foot as well," and describes a near-miss, "A shot! Oh, the crossbar saves Bolton." Close-ups of Ronaldo in his red Manchester United jersey display intense focus and determination, as the narrator explains his physical development: "His physical endurance started to last the whole match, and he started to successfully overcome the defenders' challenges." A dramatic, slightly melancholic instrumental background music begins as the narrator continues to reflect on Ronaldo's progress, stating, "The improvement was obvious, but he was still one or two years away from delivering on a very high level." The scene transitions to a still image of Sir Alex Ferguson, Carlos Queiroz, and Wayne Rooney, all smiling as Rooney holds up a Manchester United jersey with 'ROONEY 8' on the back, symbolizing a key signing. The narrator highlights these events, mentioning "the return of the assistant Carlos Queiroz and the signing of Golden Boy Wayne Rooney." The narrative then shifts to a portrait of José Mourinho in a blue-and-white Chelsea training top, set against the club’s crest, as the narrator explains the competitive landscape: "United was still far away from competing, especially with the arrival of Jose at Chelsea and the huge spending that happened there." The EPL 04/05 standings are displayed, highlighting United’s third-place finish with 77 points, while the narrator confirms, "As the story of last year was repeated, and United ended the season in third position, 18 points away from the champions Chelsea." The video then cuts to a side-by-side image of Ferguson and Arsène Wenger, followed by match highlights from the 2005 Champions League Round of 16 against AC Milan, with an on-screen graphic showing 'MILAN 1(2-0)0 UNITED MAR 8, 2005'. The narrator recounts this setback: "That was followed by an early exit in Europe against Milan in the round of 16 of the Champions League, which was a tough match for Cristiano." A brief, intense crowd roar and a distinct goal sound effect punctuate the narrative. The video then shows FA Cup semifinal highlights against Newcastle, where United triumphed 4-1, indicated by an on-screen graphic '2005 FA CUP SEMIFINALS N'CASTLE 1-4 UNITED'. The segment concludes with a close-up of Ronaldo celebrating a goal, his face beaming with joy, as the crowd roars in the background, capturing the peak of his early success at United. The narrator notes, "But the team managed to get to the FA Cup final again. This time United faced Arsenal in the final, the team that was defeated both home and away."

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with two men walking along a paved path in a green, park-like setting, as a male narrator gravely recounts a story about failing to save someone from drug abuse. The scene quickly shifts to intense close-ups of Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United 'vodafone' jersey, displaying focused and emotional expressions during matches, while the narrator connects these to personal issues affecting him that season, detailing his sending off for a dangerous challenge and an obscene gesture aimed at Benfica fans. A dramatic moment follows as a referee shows Ronaldo a red card, accompanied by a crowd roar and a whistle. The narrative then transitions to a black-and-white newspaper headline announcing Ronaldo's arrest over rape allegations, with the narrator confirming his accusation of sexual assault. The video returns to football action, showing Ronaldo dribbling and scoring in his red jersey, followed by a crowd celebration and stadium atmosphere. A stylized montage features Ronaldo celebrating with teammates, including David Beckham, against a red grid background, while on-screen graphics display his goal statistics: '44' for 02-03 and '16' for 04-05. The narrator explains how Ronaldo's insistence on showing off skills and delaying crosses affected his relationship with 'the main striker of the team,' Ruud van Nistelrooy, and how Van Nistelrooy's goal numbers took a 'huge hit,' contrasting Beckham's pinpoint crosses with Ronaldo's individual play. The segment concludes with Ronaldo in a blue training top, interacting with teammates during a training session, as the narrator continues to detail the complicated relationship between 'the Portuguese' and 'the Dutchman,' mentioning injuries as a factor.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with Manchester United players, including Cristiano Ronaldo, Gary Neville, and Wayne Rooney, in blue training tops with 'vodafone' sponsorship, on a grassy field. A male narrator immediately sets the scene, noting that "Cristiano's settling period took a very long time." The visual then transitions to a dark red graphic displaying a quote from Gary Neville, which the narrator voices, stating Neville's doubts: "I questioned Ronaldo's performance in his mid-third year. I was getting to the point where I wondered whether he could actually be a top player." The narrator then invites viewers to "watch some clips from that season," as the video shifts to a montage of grainy archival match footage from late 2005 and early 2006. This dynamic sequence showcases Ronaldo in a red Manchester United match jersey, dribbling, shooting, and scoring against various teams like Debrecen, Newcastle, Benfica, and Chelsea. During these highlights, football commentary and crowd noise emerge, with one commentator exclaiming, "And here goes old twinkle toes again!" The narrator continues, highlighting Ronaldo's "physical development" and "great ability in counter-attacks." More match action follows with commentary like "Ronaldo... slip." On-screen text introduces a match: 'UNITED - FULHAM FEB 4, 2006'. Ronaldo scores, leading to a celebration with the text 'UNITED 2-0 FULHAM RONALDO - 14’' displayed, accompanied by excited commentary: "Ronaldo, that's better, perhaps a whole lot better! It's an absolute belter!" The narrator adds that "for the first time, Cristiano successfully scoring a free kick with the same knuckleball technique that he uses today." The sequence concludes with Ronaldo celebrating another goal against Debrecen in the Champions League qualifying phase, with the scoreline 'UNITED 3-0 DEBRECEN' and his goal time '63’' on screen, as the narrator mentions he "managed to score his first European goal."

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Cristiano Ronaldo, in a red Manchester United jersey, dynamically celebrating his first ever European goal against Debrecen in the Champions League qualifiers, confirmed by the on-screen text 'UNITED 3-0 DEBRECEN RONALDO - 63’'. The narrator explains that for the third consecutive year, United were struggling and crashed out of the Champions League group stages, a struggle visually underscored by Sir Alex Ferguson animatedly gesturing on the sidelines, followed by a graphic of Group D standings showing Manchester United tied with Lille. The narrative shifts to the league, with a vintage clip of a Chelsea player scoring, as the narrator details Chelsea's dominance, lifting the Premier League with an eight-point gap over United, a fact reinforced by the 2005-06 Premier League standings graphic showing Chelsea leading. Ferguson reappears beside a blacked-out FA Cup logo and an active EFL Cup logo, signifying United's early FA Cup exit and their sole remaining chance for a title in the League Cup. Ronaldo and teammates are then seen looking intense on the bench. A tactical formation graphic, showing a 4-4-2 setup with Ronaldo on the right wing, accompanies the narrator's explanation of Ferguson's decision to start Saha, Ronaldo, and Rooney in the League Cup final. The video then shows Ronaldo scoring in the 2006 League Cup Final against Wigan, with the text 'UNITED 4-0 WIGAN FEB 26, 2006' displayed, followed by a shirtless Ronaldo celebrating with a red scarf, as the narrator confirms he added another goal in United's handsome victory. A commentator briefly interjects, stating the goal 'will treasure for the rest of his life'. The segment concludes with a detailed stats graphic for Ronaldo’s 2005-06 season, noting his age 21, as the narrator analyzes his numbers, suggesting slight improvements in goals, and summarizing the season as a 'year of development' from Ferguson's perspective, though fans were not as convinced, while Ferguson walks composedly on the pitch amidst crowd roars.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with a dynamic goal celebration by Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, scoring against Debrecen in the 2005-06 Champions League qualifying phase, with the on-screen text 'UNITED 3-0 DEBRECEN RONALDO - 63’' confirming the moment. The scene transitions to Sir Alex Ferguson, in a dark suit and red tie, animatedly gesturing on the sidelines, followed by a graphic of Group D standings in the Champions League, showing Manchester United tied with Lille on 6 points. The narrative shifts to a vintage clip of a player in a blue Chelsea jersey (number 10) scoring, then cuts to the 2005-06 Premier League standings, with Chelsea leading on 91 points and United second on 83. Ferguson reappears, this time with a blacked-out FA Cup logo and an EFL Cup logo beside him, suggesting a focus on cup competitions. A close-up shows Ronaldo and teammates on the bench, looking intense. A tactical formation graphic appears with Ferguson beside it, showing a 4-4-2 setup with Ronaldo on the right wing. The video then shows Ronaldo scoring in the 2006 League Cup Final against Wigan, with the text 'UNITED 4-0 WIGAN FEB 26, 2006' displayed, followed by a shirtless Ronaldo celebrating with a red scarf. The segment concludes with a detailed stats graphic for Ronaldo’s 2005-06 season: 33 Premier League appearances, 9 goals, 7 assists; 8 Champions League appearances, 1 goal, 1 assist; 2 FA Cup appearances, 0 goals; and 4 EFL Cup appearances, 2 goals, 1 assist, with 'AGE: 21' noted. The final shot shows Ferguson walking on the pitch, looking composed, as the crowd roars in the background.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with a dark, grid-patterned background featuring the Chelsea Football Club crest, transitioning to a graphic titled 'SUMMER 2006' listing transfers. A narrator details Chelsea's "continued spending spree" as Shevchenko (€43.88M), Kalou (€4.00M), Ballack (FREE), and Ashley Cole (€7.40M) all joined in the summer of 2006. The scene shifts to a red-toned backdrop with two men holding jerseys, as the narrator explains that Manchester United sold Van Nistelrooy to Real Madrid and Roy Keane left for Celtic in November of the previous season "after some issues with the manager." This is followed by Sir Alex Ferguson and Michael Carrick holding a Manchester United jersey with 'CARRICK 16', with the narrator introducing Carrick's arrival from Spurs. A timeline graphic appears with '03/04', '04/05', and '05/06' above Ferguson’s portrait, as the narrator describes it as a "tough summer for United," but Ferguson remained "confident" despite "failing in the league for three consecutive seasons," expecting the "young core" to "step up quickly." The atmosphere shifts from club transfer news to international football drama, introduced by a 'WORLD CUP SUMMER 2006' title card. The narrator states that "Cristiano joined the Portugal squad for the World Cup," playing in two group stage matches and scoring against the Iranian national team. This is visually supported by Portugal’s team photo, followed by footage of Ronaldo celebrating a goal against Ireland (POR 2-0 IRA, 80’), accompanied by crowd cheering and a commentator exclaiming, "And the boy from Madeira!" The narrative then moves to his injury during the Round of 16 match against the Netherlands (POR 0-0 NED, 34’), with the narrator explaining he was "subbed because of injury" during the "Battle of Nuremberg." The final frames show Ronaldo in a Portugal jersey, looking determined, with on-screen text indicating the quarterfinals match against England on July 1, 2006, as the narrator concludes that despite initial fears, Ronaldo "recovered quickly" and was a starter.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with intense action from the 2006 World Cup quarterfinals, as Wayne Rooney, in a white England jersey, commits a high tackle on a Portuguese player. The audio describes this as a 'physical battle for the ball' after an 'eventless hour,' leading to the referee, in a yellow shirt, showing Rooney a red card at the 62nd minute. Cristiano Ronaldo, in a maroon Portugal jersey, reacts with visible frustration and gestures, with the audio noting he 'came to ask for his English colleague to get sent off.' As a commentator exclaims 'tempers are flaring on the pitch,' the cameras catch Ronaldo winking to the Portuguese bench. The match eventually goes to penalties, with the audio highlighting Portugal's keeper, Ricardo, successfully saving three attempts. A penalty kick is shown where Portugal scores, giving them a 1-0 lead, and the audio confirms 'Cristiano scored the match deciding penalty.' The scoreboard updates to 'ENG 0(1-3)0 POR' at full time, indicating a 3-1 penalty shootout win for Portugal, as loud crowd noise and a commentator exclaiming 'Portugal are World Cup semi-finalists!' accompany Portuguese fans cheering wildly. A dejected England manager, Sven-Göran Eriksson, walks off. The scene transitions to a graphic of Ronaldo, contemplative, with a quote explaining his wink as a reaction to coaching staff instructions to 'push further forward to make the most of the numerical advantage.' This is followed by a newspaper-style headline, 'UGLY FACE OF SPORT' and 'PRESSURE BUILDS ON RONALDO,' reflecting the audio's narrative that the media 'wanted a scapegoat' and Ronaldo became 'the number one villain in England.' The final frame shows a serious Ronaldo as Rooney walks past with a medical bag, underscoring the controversial aftermath.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video segment opens with intense action from the 2006 World Cup quarterfinals between England and Portugal on July 1, 2006. Wayne Rooney, in a white England jersey, is shown committing a high tackle on a Portuguese player, resulting in a red card at the 62nd minute. The referee, in a yellow shirt, shows the red card as Cristiano Ronaldo, in a maroon Portugal jersey with number 17, reacts with visible frustration and gestures toward the referee. The scene shifts to Ronaldo’s perspective as he looks on, then cuts to a penalty kick where Portugal’s goalkeeper dives to his right, but the ball goes into the net, giving Portugal a 1-0 lead. The scoreboard updates to 'ENG 0(1-3)0 POR' at full time, indicating a 3-1 penalty shootout win for Portugal. The crowd erupts in celebration, with Portuguese fans cheering wildly. The camera then focuses on a dejected England manager, Sven-Göran Eriksson, walking off the pitch with a hand to his face. The segment transitions to a graphic with Ronaldo in a dark training shirt, hand on his chin, with a quote: 'They told me to push further forward to make the most of the numerical advantage and that’s how I responded.' This is followed by a newspaper-style headline: 'UGLY FACE OF SPORT' and 'PRESSURE BUILDS ON RONALDO,' with a subheading 'Friends, fall-outs, and fearsome feuds,' suggesting controversy surrounding Ronaldo. The final frame shows Ronaldo standing with hands on hips, looking serious, as England’s Wayne Rooney walks past with a medical bag, highlighting the aftermath of the red card incident.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a tense football match scene, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a maroon Portugal jersey (number 17) with hands on hips, flanked by opponents in white jerseys, including Wayne Rooney (number 9) carrying a medical bag, as a narrator explains that Ronaldo, Ferdinand, and Rooney discussed the matter, with Rooney warning Cristiano about the intense scrutiny from English fans and media that would dissect the issue throughout the summer. This narrative of media pressure is visually reinforced by a black-and-white newspaper-style graphic with the headline 'PRESSURE BUILDS ON RONALDO', featuring a dejected Ronaldo. The scene transitions to a stylized red grid background with framed headshots of Ronaldo, a player with dreadlocks, and Rooney, before cutting to Zinedine Zidane in a white France jersey (number 10) celebrating a goal, with on-screen text confirming the 2006 World Cup semifinal where Portugal lost 0-1 to France, a match after which Cristiano was voted the third best young player. The narrator reveals that following the World Cup and the loud response from the English media, Cristiano seriously considered not returning to Manchester, despite constant communication with Rooney. A split-screen of Ronaldo and Rooney is shown, followed by a man in a dark suit and sunglasses (identified by the narrator as Mendes) on the right, while Ronaldo is on the left, as the narrator explains Cristiano asked Mendes about leaving, but Mendes advised him to return to Manchester. The scene expands to include Sir Alex Ferguson and another man (possibly a coach) on the left, with a glowing white outline of Germany in the center, as the narrator details Ferguson's meeting with Mendes and Cristiano at Carlos Queiroz's house, where he spoke about David Beckham facing similar animosity in 1998. Archival footage of an England player (number 12, likely Neville) receiving a red card from a referee raising the card further illustrates the intense on-field drama. The segment concludes with Sir Alex Ferguson's voice stating, 'YOU’RE ONE OF THE BRAVEST PLAYERS TO COME TO MANCHESTER, BUT WALKING AWAY ISN'T COURAGE,' overlaid on a close-up of Ronaldo in a yellow training vest, followed by Ronaldo's own quote: 'THERE’S NO REASON FOR ME TO LEAVE A CLUB THAT HAS ALWAYS SUPPORTED ME,' as the narrator mentions Cristiano talking to the press, all set against a dramatic, tense orchestral background score.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a deep, authoritative male voice quoting Cristiano Ronaldo's dramatic statement, "THERE'S NO REASON FOR ME TO LEAVE A CLUB THAT HAS ALWAYS SUPPORTED ME," displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid background. The narrator continues, explaining that Ronaldo was staying and ready for the challenge, as the scene transitions to Ronaldo in a black Manchester United training shirt with the AIG logo, standing beside Sir Alex Ferguson and another man, followed by Ronaldo walking outdoors in a blue tracksuit. A glowing red title card, 'UNITED 2006 - 2007,' appears, setting the timeline, while the narrator confirms, "Finally, Cristiano has arrived." Electronic, slightly eerie, suspenseful music with a rhythmic beat begins as a collage of smiling faces, including Ferguson, Gary Neville, and Ronaldo, emphasizes team camaraderie. The narrator explains that in early summer training sessions, Ferguson sat with Neville, Rooney, and Ronaldo, instantly feeling the team was united and creating a sense that the "entire world was against Manchester United." Visually, Ronaldo is seen in a yellow training vest conversing with Ferguson on a pitch, then in match footage from the 2006-2007 Premier League opener against Fulham, wearing a red Manchester United jersey. A man in a suit is briefly shown on the pitch, followed by Ronaldo in a black training top standing in the rain beside Wayne Rooney, both in AIG-branded gear. A newspaper headline, 'Real boss Capello keen on Ronaldo,' appears, aligning with the narrator's discussion of the opening match receiving immense media attention, with every step and reaction from Rooney and Cristiano analyzed, and media painting a false picture of them "not getting along." The narrator adds that the open transfer window fueled speculation, as the video concludes with Ronaldo scoring a powerful goal, celebrating with a raised arm, and a close-up highlighting his focused performance.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo in a white Manchester United away jersey, screaming in celebration as a commentator exclaims, "And he finds the net with a brilliant shot!" A narrator then explains how Ronaldo's talent exploded in November and December 2006, leading him to win back-to-back Barclays Player of the Month awards, a feat highlighted by a split-screen graphic and not achieved since Dennis Bergkamp in '97 and Robbie Fowler in '95-'96. The narrator continues, detailing Ronaldo's impressive streak of scoring a brace in three consecutive matches against Aston Villa, Wigan, and Reading, as the respective club crests appear. Live match footage then shows Ronaldo, in his red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship, scoring twice in a 3-2 victory over Reading in the 06/07 Premier League, with an excited commentator confirming, "third game running, Cristiano Ronaldo has scored two goals!" The narrative shifts to a crucial away match against Fulham on February 24, 2007, where, as the narrator recounts, United were in danger of dropping points at Craven Cottage. In the 88th minute, Ronaldo stepped up, deciding the match with a great solo effort. The footage captures his powerful run, shot, and jubilant celebration, with a commentator's electrifying call, "Ronaldo's away this time... he's three up with it... Ronaldo goes! And he scores the goal!" as the crowd roars. The scene concludes with Ronaldo in a focused side profile and a wide-angle view of the pitch, emphasizing the dramatic late winner.

Ronaldo and teammates, including Smith (number 14), are then seen clapping the crowd in celebration. Action footage follows, showing Ronaldo scoring a goal against Roma in the 06/07 Champions League quarterfinals, with on-screen text marking his goals at 44' and 49', as the narrator explains Cristiano and United were performing on a high level, scoring seven goals against Roma, with Cristiano scoring a brace and assisting another. A commentator's excited shout of "Surely seals it!" and "His first goal in the Champions League proper!" accompanies the goal, confirming it was his first two goals in the Champions League. The sequence continues with Ronaldo scoring against Milan in the semifinals (3-2, 1st leg, April 24, 2007), followed by a close-up of him celebrating with a fierce expression, as the narrator details United facing Milan in the semifinals, winning the first leg 3-2. The final clip shows Ronaldo in a white away kit during the 2nd leg against Milan (2-3, May 2, 2007), with a focused look as he runs on the pitch, as the narrator mentions Cristiano struggling in the San Siro.

A wide-angle view shows the ball narrowly missing the net, followed by Ronaldo's frustrated expression. The scoreboard then reveals United's dramatic defeat: 'Milan 3 (5-3) 0 United FULL TIME', with the narrator confirming "United lost by three goals to nil and were out of the competition." The scene transitions to a celebratory moment, with Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, raising his arms in triumph after scoring a goal, accompanied by crowd cheering and an excited Arabic commentator, followed by a close-up of his focused face. segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a stylized graphic of Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, giving a thumbs-up, with on-screen text indicating goal tallies from 'AUG 07-JAN 08'. A narrator explains that Ronaldo started scoring 'all kinds of goals' and 'acquiring a nose for goal,' surpassing his previous season's tally. The scene transitions to live match footage, where Ronaldo, in a red AIG-sponsored jersey, scores his 'first header of the season' against Wigan Athletic, as confirmed by on-screen text '07/08 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW9 UNITED 4-0 WIGAN OCT 6, 2007'. The electric atmosphere is palpable with a crowd roar, and a commentator exclaims, 'Oh my word!' and later, 'A header from Cristiano Ronaldo!' Ronaldo celebrates with teammates, including Saha. The narrator notes that this goal 'came as a surprise for Cristiano.' He celebrates with arms raised. In the following month, Ronaldo scores a 'fantastic header' against Blackburn Rovers on November 11, 2007, celebrating with arms outstretched. The narrator highlights that this time, it 'was not a surprise,' as 'headers were now a big part of Cristiano's arsenal of attributes.' A match against Fulham in December showcases Ronaldo's 'wide range of goals,' as he scores twice. The first is described by the narrator as a 'beautiful and instinctive volley,' and the second as a header 'any striker would be proud of.' On-screen text confirms the goals at 10' and 58'. He celebrates with teammates, including Giggs. The crowd erupts again, with a commentator shouting, 'Ronaldo! He can't stop scoring!' The final clip shows Ronaldo scoring a goal in a match against Chelsea, celebrating with arms raised in a packed stadium, surrounded by advertising boards for brands like Heineken and PlayStation, underscoring the high-stakes environment. The narrator concludes by mentioning Ronaldo's skill in cutting from the right to the left and shooting, observed in another match.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dynamic action shot of Cristiano Ronaldo, in a red Manchester United jersey, scoring a goal against Everton on December 23, 2007, as on-screen text confirms '07/08 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW18 UNITED 2-1 EVERTON'. A narrator describes this as a goal scored "with his weak foot from range," questioning, "Is there anything this man can't do?" Ronaldo celebrates with a powerful, clenched-fist gesture as the crowd roars. The scene transitions to a stylized graphic of the 2007 Ballon d'Or results, visually showing Ronaldo in first place with 444 votes, flanked by Kaka (2nd, 277 votes) and Lionel Messi (3rd, 255 votes). The narrator, however, states that Ronaldo's "huge development in the year 2007 earned him the second position in the Ballon d'Or rankings behind Kaka and just ahead of Messi," adding that he was "very close to the top" but "still needed to win more titles," specifically "a title like the Champions League." The narrative then shifts to a Champions League match against Lyon on March 4, 2008, where Ronaldo, in a white away kit, scores a goal and celebrates with teammates, with the narrator noting Cristiano "sealed the tie against Lyon." United then faced Roma in the Champions League quarterfinal on April 1, 2008. The narrator explains that "Scholes lifted the ball up high as Ronaldo soared from far away to score a beautiful and important header," which is seen as Ronaldo scores in the 39th minute, confirmed by on-screen text 'ROMA 0-1 UNITED RONALDO - 39''. An excited commentator shouts "Ronaldo!" during the celebration, followed by a wide-angle replay and a close-up of Wayne Rooney waving to the crowd. The narrator concludes this section by stating United "passed this tough test and got to another semi-final in the Champions League." The video then presents a split-screen graphic contrasting Manchester United and FC Barcelona, with Ronaldo and Messi, respectively, as the narrator announces United "then faced Barcelona, and it was the first official match between Cristiano and Messi." This is followed by a tactical formation graphic showing the starting lineups for both teams, with Ronaldo and Tevez leading United's attack, and Messi and Eto'o for Barcelona. The narrator mentions that "Carlos Queiroz suggested the change in tactics before the match for Cristiano, as the Portuguese started the tie as a striker," before the audio cuts off. The segment concludes with a close-up of Park Ji-sung looking focused, as the tactical diagram remains on screen.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a split-screen graphic, visually contrasting a young J. S. Park in a red Manchester United jersey with a tactical formation of FC Barcelona, as a commentator explains how 'Park Ji-sung' was used to cover Barcelona's right side and specifically mark Messi. The scene transitions to the 2007/08 Champions League semifinal first leg, where Cristiano Ronaldo, in a white away kit, takes a penalty that is saved by the goalkeeper, with the commentator exclaiming, 'Oh, this is the one he has missed!' The audio notes Ronaldo 'struggled for possession' and 'missed an early and crucial penalty.' The narrative shifts to the second leg at Old Trafford, where Paul Scholes, in a red United jersey, scores a 'long-range effort' in the 14th minute, prompting the commentator to shout, 'What about that!' and the crowd to erupt in celebration, as Manchester United secured their place in the Champions League final. The video then cuts to the 2007/08 Premier League match against Wigan, where Ronaldo, now in a red home kit, scores a penalty in the 32nd minute, described by the commentator as 'as cool as you like!' The audio highlights Ronaldo's continued scoring and the 'race for the league title' heating up, leading to United's 2-0 victory. The final scene shows Ronaldo and teammates joyfully celebrating with fans amidst falling confetti, as the commentator notes 'Cristiano lifted the new title,' concluding with 'Sustaining success.'
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a split-screen graphic showing a young J. S. Park in a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship on the left, and a tactical formation of FC Barcelona on the right, featuring players like Eto'o, Messi, Iniesta, Xavi, Deco, Toure, Abidal, Milito, Marquez, Zambrotta, and Valdes. The scene transitions to live match footage from the 2007/08 Champions League semifinal first leg between Barcelona and Manchester United on April 23, 2008, where Cristiano Ronaldo, in a white away kit with 'AIG' sponsorship, takes a penalty kick that is saved by the goalkeeper, leading to a 0-0 draw. The narrative then shifts to the second leg at Old Trafford on April 29, 2008, where Paul Scholes scores in the 14th minute, giving United a 1-0 lead, and the crowd erupts in celebration. The match ends 1-0 to United, with Ronaldo visibly emotional. The video then cuts to the 2007/08 Premier League match against Wigan on May 11, 2008, where Ronaldo, now in a red home kit, scores a penalty in the 32nd minute, and later celebrates with teammates after a 2-0 victory. The final scene shows Ronaldo and teammates joyfully celebrating with fans, wearing red jerseys and scarves, as confetti falls, marking the culmination of a successful season.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a celebratory scene of Manchester United players, including Cristiano Ronaldo, lifting the Premier League trophy adorned with a 'Barclays' ribbon, amidst a jubilant crowd, as a commentator remarks on the victory being "so hard, but so, so sweet." A calm, informative narrator with a non-native English accent then sets the scene for the 2008 Champions League Final between Manchester United and Chelsea, confirming the match details with on-screen text. Tactical formations for both teams are displayed, with the narrator detailing Ronaldo's role on the left side, given freedom to roam and exploit his speed against Chelsea's defense, which included Michael Essien as a right-back. The narrator continues to explain the tight marking on Cristiano by players like Essien, Ballack, and Joe Cole, but highlights his development in overcoming this. The action shifts to the pitch where Ronaldo, in his red 'AIG' Manchester United jersey, skillfully dribbles past a Chelsea defender and scores a powerful shot, leading to a massive crowd roar and an excited commentator shouting "Ronaldo!", with on-screen text confirming 'UNITED 1-0 CHELSEA RONALDO - 26''. Chelsea's Frank Lampard then equalizes in the 45th minute, as shown by the text 'UNITED 1-1 CHELSEA LAMPARD - 45'', a moment the narrator explicitly mentions. The narrator explains the match eventually went to a penalty shootout, and after two successful penalties for both sides, Cristiano stepped up to shoot. The video then shows Ronaldo attempting a penalty shot that is saved by Chelsea's goalkeeper, Petr Cech, prompting the excited commentator to exclaim, "And Cech has saved from Ronaldo of all people!" The final frames depict Ronaldo in the heavy rain, visibly frustrated and dejected after the missed penalty, with fans holding umbrellas in the background.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with a dramatic orchestral score as a male narrator recounts the tense 2008 Champions League Final, describing Terry's slip on the crucial fifth penalty and Van der Sar's save of Anelka's shot, which helped Manchester United lift the trophy. Visually, Cristiano Ronaldo, in a rain-soaked red Manchester United jersey, appears distraught after a missed opportunity, followed by dejected Chelsea players and a frustrated goalkeeper. The scene then erupts into celebration, with jubilant crowd cheers and a commentator exclaiming, "English night in Europe is Manchester United's night!" as Ronaldo lifts the Champions League trophy amidst confetti and continuing rain, embracing Sir Alex Ferguson and smiling broadly. The narrator continues, highlighting Cristiano's achievement of lifting the title, which could secure him the Ballon d'Or, marking a "double for Cristiano" and "another outstanding season" for the Portuguese star. A graphic displays Ronaldo’s impressive 2007-2008 stats, showing 31 Premier League goals and 42 overall, as the narrator confirms he won the European Golden Boot award, adding that fans are excited for another season. The dramatic music transitions to a suspenseful, electronic sound as a title card 'EUROS AND THE 6TH SEASON 2007-2008' appears. The narrator then quotes Carlos Queiroz, displayed over a red grid with his image, stating to Ferguson in the past that "if you manage to get five years out of Cristiano, then you've struck gold," and that "there is no precedent for a Portuguese teenager to go to another country and stay for five years." The segment concludes with Ronaldo smiling beside Ferguson, both wearing medals, as the narrator mentions hints of Cristiano leaving, referencing his quote, displayed over a red grid with his image, "I have said millions of times that I would love to play in Spain," as he "dreamed of playing in Spain" before the Champions League final.
segments.visual_original_caption: The video opens with a dramatic moment from the 2008 Champions League Final, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, visibly distraught after a missed opportunity, with rain soaking his jersey and the net in the foreground. The scene quickly shifts to a close-up of a Chelsea player in a blue jersey, looking dejected, followed by a goalkeeper in green raising his arm in frustration. The narrative then transitions to a celebratory montage: Ronaldo, still in his red Manchester United jersey, is shown lifting the Champions League trophy amidst a crowd of jubilant teammates and fans, with confetti falling and rain continuing to fall. He is seen embracing Sir Alex Ferguson, both wearing medals, and later celebrating with a wide smile, holding the trophy close. The video then cuts to a graphic displaying Ronaldo’s 2007-2008 season stats: 34 Premier League appearances with 31 goals and 9 assists, 11 Champions League appearances with 8 goals and 1 assist, and 3 FA Cup appearances with 3 goals. This is followed by a dynamic shot of Ronaldo celebrating a goal with a clenched fist, his jersey bearing the 'AIG' sponsorship. The segment concludes with a title card reading 'EUROS AND THE 6TH SEASON 2007-2008' in glowing red text, followed by a quote from Carlos Queiroz: 'IF YOU GET FIVE YEARS OUT OF HIM, YOU’VE STRUCK GOLD,' and then 'THERE’S NO PRECEDENT FOR IT,' both displayed over a dark red grid background with Queiroz’s image. The final frames show Ronaldo smiling beside Ferguson, both wearing medals, and a quote from Ronaldo himself: 'I HAVE SAID MILLIONS OF TIMES THAT I WOULD LOVE TO PLAY IN SPAIN,' displayed over a red grid background with his image.
