segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Cristiano Ronaldo, in a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and the number 7, joyfully spraying champagne from a bottle as he celebrates with teammates, including Ferdinand, on a green pitch under bright stadium lights, while a calm male narrator discusses his upcoming complicated season with United. The scene transitions to a smiling, rain-soaked Ronaldo beside Sir Alex Ferguson, who is wearing a dark polo shirt with the Portuguese national team crest, as the narrator introduces the Euros in the summer. A group shot of the Portuguese national team, including Ronaldo in red jerseys with green trim, is shown, with the narrator explaining Ronaldo was now 'the main man of the team' and co-captain. A graphic of the UEFA Euro 2008 Group A standings appears, showing Portugal as group leaders, but the narrator states they faced the Germans in the next round. A match-up graphic pitting Portugal against Germany precedes live match footage of a German player scoring, followed by Ronaldo reacting with frustration on the pitch. The final score 'POR 2-3 GER' is displayed, indicating Portugal's quarterfinal loss, with an excited male commentator exclaiming, 'Germany were on their way to the semi-finals!' The narrator then explains that despite 'rumors surrounding Ronaldo,' Ferguson traveled to Portugal to meet him, refusing to sell him to Real Madrid, emphasizing his desire to maintain his 'authority in the global stage.' The segment concludes with a series of split-screen graphics featuring Ferguson, Ronaldo, and other figures, followed by a close-up of Ferguson in a white shirt with the Manchester United crest, and finally a quote from his autobiography: 'IF SOMEONE COMES, AND OFFERS A WORLD RECORD FEE, THEN DON'T MESS US ABOUT,' as the narrator references Ferguson's autobiography and his conditions for Ronaldo's future.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a quote from Sir Alex Ferguson, displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid, stating, 'AND SOMEONE COMES AND OFFERS A WORLD RECORD FEE, THEN WE WILL LET YOU GO,' accompanied by Ferguson’s thoughtful portrait. A calm male narrator explains that Cristiano had agreed to be sold if a record offer came. The scene transitions to a smiling Cristiano Ronaldo and Ferguson seated together at an event, both in formal suits. The narrator continues, detailing that despite this, it was Ronaldo's weakest season due to a nagging injury since March 2008, as a graphic overlay of 'MARCH 2008' appears over Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, first in a close-up, then in a split-screen celebrating, and finally in action on the pitch. The injury worsened after a match against Germany in the Euros, leading to surgery on his ankle and six weeks out of action, visually depicted by Ronaldo lying on the grass in pain after a tackle and later walking with crutches in a white t-shirt and black shorts. Ronaldo is then shown in a black Manchester United training jersey with the number 10, looking intense, as the narrator states he returned in September with a clear goal to seal the Ballon d'Or. Match footage shows Ronaldo scoring a goal and celebrating with teammates, accompanied by an excited football commentator exclaiming, "First start for four months, first goal of the season!" The narrator notes that his celebrations were subdued, and he began to distance himself from teammates. A quote from Gary Neville then appears, overlaid on his portrait in a red jersey, stating, 'HE WAS VERY PROFESSIONAL THAT WHOLE SEASON,' followed by 'BUT HE WAS COUNTING DOWN THE MONTHS UNTIL HE COULD LEAVE,' with the narrator attributing these words to Neville. The segment concludes with Ronaldo in a red jersey, looking frustrated after a match, rain visibly soaking his jersey, and a final close-up of his determined expression, as the narrator remarks that despite everything, Cristiano eventually got back to his best.

segments.integrated_caption: The video opens with an inspiring orchestral score as a narrator details Cristiano Ronaldo's impressive form, leading to his 100th goal for Manchester United in November. Visually, Ronaldo, in his red 'AIG' sponsored Manchester United jersey, celebrates his goal with a wide smile and raised arms, accompanied by a commentator's excited shout, "Cristiano Ronaldo brings up 100 goals for Manchester United!" and roaring crowd noise. A title card confirms the '08/09 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW13 UNITED 5-0 STOKE' match and 'CRISTIANO'S 100TH UNITED GOAL'. The scene transitions to Ronaldo in a dark suit, affectionately kissing the golden Ballon d'Or trophy in front of a 'FRANCE football' backdrop. The narrator explains that the Ballon d'Or winner was announced in December, with no doubt Ronaldo was the most deserving, winning by a big margin ahead of Lionel Messi and Fernando Torres, as shown by a graphic displaying '2008 BALLON D'OR' results with '1ST 446', '2ND 281', and '3RD 129'. Ronaldo's ambition is highlighted through on-screen quotes, narrated by the speaker: "THIS TROPHY IS SO IMPORTANT THAT I WANT TO WIN IT AGAIN" and "I’LL WAKE UP EVERY DAY AND SAY TO MYSELF, “I HAVE TO KEEP IMPROVING.”" The video then cuts back to match action, with Ronaldo in his red jersey, scoring and celebrating in the 2008 FIFA Club World Cup semifinals against Osaka, confirmed by a title card 'OSAKA 3-5 UNITED DEC 18, 2008', with a commentator exclaiming, "There it is! It is Ronaldo!" The narrator adds that he also provided an assist in the final. Further crucial goals are shown: one against Inter in the 2009 Champions League Round of 16, with a title card 'UNITED 2 (2-0) 0 INTER MAR 11, 2009' and a commentator declaring, "United heading for the quarter-finals!", and finally, an outrageous long-range effort against Porto in the quarterfinals, with a title card 'PORTO 0 (2-3) 1 UNITED APR 15, 2009'. The triumphant atmosphere is underscored by the continuous background music and crowd reactions.

segments.integrated_caption: The segment opens with Cristiano Ronaldo, wearing a red Manchester United jersey, scoring a dramatic goal against Arsenal in the 2009 Champions League semifinal, a moment an English commentator exclaims is "Absolutely sensational!" This leads to a 3-1 aggregate victory for Manchester United. The scene transitions to Ronaldo celebrating joyfully with teammates and family, holding the Barclays Premier League trophy, as a narrator details his achievements: winning "the league, the League Cup, and the Club World Cup," accompanied by celebratory crowd sounds. The narrative then shifts to the 2009 Champions League Final against Barcelona, with the narrator noting it was Ronaldo's "second Champions League final in a row" and that he "made life hard for Victor Valdes in the opening minutes." An excited Arabic commentator shouts Ronaldo's name during early action. However, the match turns as the narrator explains Pep Guardiola "changed the match" by pushing Eto'o to the right and Messi as a full striker, leading Barcelona to "take over possession of the ball" and score two goals. As Messi's goal is shown, a highly energetic Arabic commentator describes it as a "dangerous ball" and "the bullet of mercy from Messi!" The match ends in a 2-0 defeat for United, with Ronaldo shown covering his face in emotional disappointment. The referee signals the end, followed by Barcelona players, including Messi, celebrating their victory, with an English commentator stating, "There it is!" amidst crowd cheers.

segments.integrated_caption: The minute opens with a wide shot of a football stadium at night, where Cristiano Ronaldo, in a white Manchester United away jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and red boots, stands pensive with hands on hips, as an Arabic commentator excitedly mentions "Manchester" and "FC Barcelona." The camera zooms in for a close-up of his serious, focused expression before cutting to a black screen. A graphic split-screen then appears, showing Ronaldo on the left and Florentino Pérez (labeled 'CALDERON') on the right, flanked by the Manchester United and Real Madrid crests and a prominent '€94.00M' transfer fee. A melancholic, slightly tense background music begins as an English narrator explains that "all indicators pointed to Cristiano playing his last match for United," especially after "Calderon stepped down and Perez took over the presidency of Real Madrid," with "the Merengue" making a "record offer." The visuals shift to a daytime street scene with a crowd of fans gathered outside a stadium, followed by a shot of a white car driving through the crowd, then a close-up of a man in a suit and glasses. The scene cuts to a young Ronaldo in a black-and-white striped youth jersey playing football on a dirt pitch, representing his early career. The narrator continues, marking "a new page in Cristiano's story," as the music becomes more hopeful. A stadium filled with fans is shown, aligning with the narration of "Madrid fans" filling the "Santiago Bernabéu," excited for Ronaldo to "end Barcelona's reign of dominance" and make Madrid "European champions." Ronaldo is then seen in a red Manchester United jersey celebrating a goal with his arms raised, as the narrator concludes that he "finally got what he wanted" after "years of hard work" and his "strive to be the best." The final shot shows Ronaldo in a white jersey with the number 9 on his back, running down a tunnel toward bright stadium lights ahead, as the narrator poses the question, "So what does the future hold for the Portuguese star?"
